stem: A right circular cone has a volume of 20,328π cubic centimeters and the area of its base is 1,089π square centimeters. What is the slant height, in centimeters, of this cone?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The base area πr² = 1,089π gives r = 33. The volume formula V = (1/3)πr²h gives 20,328π = (1/3)(1,089π)h, so h = 56. The slant height is √(r² + h²) = √(1,089 + 3,136) = √4,225 = 65.

Choice A gives the radius, choice C gives the height, choice D adds r and h.
